overview: The image appears to show a Tortoise Bont Tick (Amblyomma marmoreum), a species of hard tick commonly found on tortoises. These ticks possess distinctive ornate markings on their bodies, often described as white or cream-colored patterns against a reddish-brown background. As parasites, they feed on the blood of their hosts, which can include various reptiles and occasionally mammals. They are known to be vectors for certain pathogens, including those that cause heartwater disease in livestock. iNaturalist +3 4 sites Tortoise Bont Tick (Amblyomma marmoreum) · iNaturalist

The image appears to show a Tortoise Bont Tick (Amblyomma marmoreum), a species of hard tick commonly found on tortoises.
These ticks possess distinctive ornate markings on their bodies, often described as white or cream-colored patterns against a reddish-brown background.
As parasites, they feed on the blood of their hosts, which can include various reptiles and occasionally mammals.
They are known to be vectors for certain pathogens, including those that cause heartwater disease in livestock.
